_description: ContentForge Social Platform Specifications — Character limits, hashtag norms, voice guidelines, image/video specs, AI-disclosure requirements, and supported post types per platform. Used by the Social Adapter Agent (10-social-adapter) and cf-social-adapt skill. Platform limits verified July 2026.
_posting_times_note: Posting-time folklore removed (July 2026); the old best_times/worst_times tables were unsourced and timezone-free. Use the brand's own platform analytics to determine posting windows.

hashtags.notes: Use industry-specific terms. Avoid overly generic hashtags. CamelCase for readability.

voice.notes: Write like a knowledgeable peer, not a press release. Conversational authority.

format.truncation_note: LinkedIn shows roughly 140 (mobile) to 210 (desktop) characters before 'see more'. Hook must be in first 2 lines.

ai_disclosure.mechanism: none native — disclose manually in post copy (e.g., 'Image: AI-generated')

linkedin.algorithm_notes: External links may reduce reach. Consider putting link in first comment. Native documents (carousels) get higher distribution.

twitter._limit_note: 280 characters for standard accounts; X Premium accounts can post up to 25,000 characters. Default adaptations should target the 280 limit unless the brand profile confirms a Premium account — and even then, short posts get better feed treatment.

hashtags._note: X has algorithmically deprioritized hashtags through 2025-26; zero hashtags is now a legitimate default. Use at most 1-2 only when a specific tag has an active audience.
hashtags.notes: Hashtags consume character budget. Use only high-value, specific hashtags. Never in mid-sentence.

voice.notes: Every word must earn its place. One idea per tweet. Be sharp.

format.truncation_note: URLs always count as 23 characters regardless of actual length.

ai_disclosure.mechanism: none native — disclose manually in post copy

twitter.algorithm_notes: Replies and quote tweets boost distribution. Images increase engagement by ~35%. Threads get more sustained visibility than single tweets.

hashtags.notes: Mix broad reach hashtags with niche discovery hashtags. Avoid banned hashtags. Can go in first comment to keep caption clean.

voice.notes: The visual does the heavy lifting. Caption adds context and personality. Write like you are talking to a friend who is interested in the topic.

format.truncation_note: Instagram shows ~125 characters before 'more'. First line is critical.

reel._note: Reels support up to 3 minutes (raised from 90s in early 2025). Stories remain 60s per card.

ai_disclosure.mechanism: Meta 'AI info' label — auto-applied to detected synthetic media; disclose proactively via the AI label toggle when posting photorealistic AI content

instagram.algorithm_notes: Carousels get highest engagement. Saves and shares weight more than likes. Reels get broader distribution than feed posts. Alt text improves accessibility and may boost SEO.

hashtags.notes: Hashtags are less important on Facebook. Use 1-3 relevant ones. Do not overdo it.

voice.notes: Write for a broad audience. Stories and questions outperform link dumps. Make people feel invited to participate.

format.truncation_note: Facebook truncates around 477 characters with 'See more'. Hook in first paragraph.

ai_disclosure.mechanism: Meta 'AI info' label — auto-applied to detected synthetic media; disclose proactively when posting photorealistic AI content

facebook.algorithm_notes: Comments weight heavily in algorithm. Questions and polls drive comments. Native images and video outperform external links. Engagement bait ('tag a friend') is penalized.

hashtags.notes: Threads culture is less hashtag-driven. Keep it natural. 1-3 relevant tags max (Threads 'topic tags' render without the # symbol).

voice.notes: Write like texting a smart colleague. Hot takes, quick insights, and genuine questions perform best. Authenticity over polish.

format.truncation_note: No truncation at 500 chars. Full post visible.

ai_disclosure.mechanism: Meta 'AI info' label (same system as Instagram/Facebook)

threads.algorithm_notes: Replies and conversation threads get amplified. Text-only posts perform well. Images optional but not required. Authenticity and opinion drive engagement.

tiktok._limit_note: Caption limit for video posts and photo posts. Keep the first ~100 characters hook-worthy; captions truncate in feed.

hashtags.notes: Mix one broad tag, 2-3 niche/topic tags, and optionally one branded tag. TikTok search behaves like a search engine — keyword-rich captions matter as much as hashtags.

voice.notes: Lead with the payoff in the first second. Text overlays carry the message for sound-off viewers. Polished brand voice underperforms creator-style delivery.

format.truncation_note: Feed shows roughly the first 1-2 lines of caption before 'more'.

feed_video._note: Uploads up to 10 minutes supported; short (under 60s) videos still get the widest distribution.

photo_post._note: TikTok photo posts (carousel photo mode) support up to 35 images with music. Strong format for step-by-step and listicle repurposing.

ai_disclosure.mechanism: TikTok 'AI-generated content' toggle at upload — required by TikTok policy for realistic synthetic media; unlabeled AI content risks removal

tiktok.algorithm_notes: Completion rate and rewatches dominate distribution. Photo posts get carousel-style swipe engagement. Native text overlays and trending audio boost reach. Watermarked reposts from other platforms are penalized.

hashtags.notes: Hashtags are clickable but carry no native algorithmic boost — discovery runs on custom feeds and keyword search. Write for search terms, not tags.

avoid: cross-posted X threads with X conventions
voice.notes: Bluesky culture is anti-broadcast and skeptical of brands. Conversational, useful, and self-aware posts earn replies; hard selling gets muted.

format.truncation_note: No mid-post truncation at 300 chars. Links render as social cards (title + image pulled from OG tags) and do not consume extra characters beyond the URL text.

ai_disclosure.mechanism: none native — disclose manually in post copy or alt text

bluesky.algorithm_notes: No single master algorithm — users subscribe to custom feeds. Getting picked up by topical feeds (via keywords) matters more than posting time. Alt text is strongly normed; posts without it get called out.

youtube_shorts._limit_note: Title caps at 100 characters. Description allows 5,000 but only the first ~100 characters show without expansion — front-load the hook and keywords.

ideal_length._note: Applies to the description. Titles should be 40-70 characters for full display on all surfaces.

hashtags.notes: #Shorts is no longer required for classification. Use 1-3 topical tags; YouTube search keywords in the title and description matter far more.

avoid: clickbait that the video doesn't pay off
voice.notes: The title is the hook. Front-load the topic keyword for search. Shorts viewers decide in under 2 seconds.

format.truncation_note: Description truncates at ~100 visible characters; links below the fold still count for click-through from the expanded view.

short._note: Shorts support up to 3 minutes (raised from 60s in October 2024). Under-60s Shorts still see the strongest swipe-feed distribution.

ai_disclosure.mechanism: YouTube 'altered or synthetic content' disclosure at upload — required for realistic AI-generated or altered media; YouTube may add the label itself if undisclosed

youtube_shorts.algorithm_notes: Swipe-through rate and viewed-vs-swiped ratio drive distribution. Shorts feed traffic dwarfs search for this format, but titles still index in YouTube search. Loopable endings increase rewatch rate.

hashtag_tiers._description: Hashtag categorization framework used across all platforms

tier_1_broad.description: High volume, high competition industry hashtags

tier_2_specific.description: Medium volume, medium competition topic hashtags

tier_3_niche.description: Low volume, low competition, high relevance hashtags
tier_3_niche.use_case: Niche audience engagement, thought leadership positioning

post_frameworks._description: Five core post frameworks referenced by templates/social-post-templates.md
post_frameworks.announcement: Announce a key finding or trend with context and CTA
post_frameworks.data_driven_insight: Lead with a specific statistic, provide analysis, end with implication
post_frameworks.how_to_tip: Practical step-by-step value the reader can act on immediately
post_frameworks.quote_highlight: Feature a quotable line with attribution and commentary
post_frameworks.story_case_study: Narrative arc: problem, solution, result with real-world proof
